revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{lua|Module:Flag}} {{Expensive}} == Description == {{flag+link|string|country|variant|size=|name=}} Produces a flag icon attached to a wikilinked article. This template is similar to the more widely used {{tl|flag}} and {{tl|flag country}} templates, but is more useful for representing something like a country/region/place/organization/etc followed by a link, labelled using that country/region/etc's name, to an article on some aspect of that country/region/etc. ;Parameters *{{para|1}} (first unnamed parameter): Use a string that will precede the country name defined in the second parameter. *{{para|2}} (second unnamed parameter): Use either the name of the state or in some cases, a three-letter country code (e.g. [[ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]]) *{{para|variant}} (optional parameter): Specifies an alternative (historical) flag to use. This parameter is documented by the appropriate template in [[:Category:Country data templates]]. *{{para|size}} (optional): Can be used to change the flag icon size. *{{para|name}} (optional): The display text of the target wikilink is usually automatically generated, but can always be over-ridden with the name parameter. ==Usage examples== # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} #:In this case, the country code for Liechtenstein (LIE) is used to produce the correct flag and wikilink to [[Politics of Liechtenstein]]. # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} #:Similar to the first example, but with a flag variant parameter to select a historical flag image. #{{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} → {{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} #:Optionally, an alternate name can be displayed. Note that the wikilink still uses the name used in the country's article (defined in [[Template:Country data Czech Republic]] in this case). #{{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} → {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} #:If no article exists with the name formed by the combination of ''string'' and ''name'', the link will default to [[''name'']]. {{tlx|flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}}, for instance, produces {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} with "North Korea" as a link to [[North Korea]] because [[Disc golf in North Korea]] does not exist. To specify a width for the icon and/or a particular variant/version of the flag, set the parameters {{para|size}} and/or {{para|variant}} in the same manner as {{tl|flag icon}}'s ''size'' and ''variant'' parameters. (See [[Template:Flag icon/doc|{{tlf|flag icon}}'s documentation]] for more details.) ===Example use in articles=== For examples of this template's use in articles, see the following articles: * [[Cabbage#Production]] * [[Administrative divisions of South America#Table]] (leftmost columns) {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{lua|Module:Flag}} {{Expensive}} == Description == {{flag+link|string|country|variant|size=|name=}} Produces a flag icon attached to a wikilinked article. This template is similar to the more widely used {{tl|flag}} and {{tl|flag country}} templates, but is more useful for representing something like a country/region/place/organization/etc followed by a link, labelled using that country/region/etc's name, to an article on some aspect of that country/region/etc. ;Parameters *{{para|1}} (first unnamed parameter): Use a string that will precede the country name defined in the second parameter. *{{para|2}} (second unnamed parameter): Use either the name of the state or in some cases, a three-letter country code (e.g. [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]]) *{{para|variant}} (optional parameter): Specifies an alternative (historical) flag to use. This parameter is documented by the appropriate template in [[:Category:Country data templates]]. *{{para|size}} (optional): Can be used to change the flag icon size. *{{para|name}} (optional): The display text of the target wikilink is usually automatically generated, but can always be over-ridden with the name parameter. ==Usage examples== # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} #:In this case, the country code for Liechtenstein (LIE) is used to produce the correct flag and wikilink to [[Politics of Liechtenstein]]. # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} #:Similar to the first example, but with a flag variant parameter to select a historical flag image. #{{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} → {{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} #:Optionally, an alternate name can be displayed. Note that the wikilink still uses the name used in the country's article (defined in [[Template:Country data Czech Republic]] in this case). #{{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} → {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} #:If no article exists with the name formed by the combination of ''string'' and ''name'', the link will default to [[''name'']]. {{tlx|flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}}, for instance, produces {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} with "North Korea" as a link to [[North Korea]] because [[Disc golf in North Korea]] does not exist. To specify a width for the icon and/or a particular variant/version of the flag, set the parameters {{para|size}} and/or {{para|variant}} in the same manner as {{tl|flag icon}}'s ''size'' and ''variant'' parameters. (See [[Template:Flag icon/doc|{{tlf|flag icon}}'s documentation]] for more details.) ===Example use in articles=== For examples of this template's use in articles, see the following articles: * [[Cabbage#Production]] * [[Administrative divisions of South America#Table]] (leftmost columns) {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{lua|Module:Flag}} {{Expensive}} == Description == {{flag+link|string|country|variant|size=|name=}} Produces a flag icon attached to a wikilinked article. This template is similar to the more widely used {{tl|flag}} and {{tl|flag country}} templates, but is more useful for representing something like a country/region/place/organization/etc followed by a link, labelled using that country/region/etc's name, to an article on some aspect of that country/region/etc. ;Parameters *{{para|1}} (first unnamed parameter): Use a string that will precede the country name defined in the second parameter. *{{para|2}} (second unnamed parameter): Use either the name of the state or in some cases, a three-letter country code (e.g. [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]]) *{{para|variant}} (optional parameter): Specifies an alternative (historical) flag to use. This parameter is documented by the appropriate template in [[:Category:Country data templates]]. *{{para|size}} (optional): Can be used to change the flag icon size. *{{para|name}} (optional): The display text of the target wikilink is usually automatically generated, but can always be over-ridden with the name parameter. ==Usage examples== # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} #:In this case, the country code for Liechtenstein (LIE) is used to produce the correct flag and wikilink to [[Politics of Liechtenstein]]. # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} #:Similar to the first example, but with a flag variant parameter to select a historical flag image. #{{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} → {{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} #:Optionally, an alternate name can be displayed. Note that the wikilink still uses the name used in the country's article (defined in [[Template:Country data Czech Republic]] in this case). #{{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} → {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} #:If no article exists with the name formed by the combination of ''string'' and ''name'', the link will default to [[''name'']]. {{tlx|flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}}, for instance, produces {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} with "North Korea" as a link to [[:en:North Korea|North Korea]] because [[Disc golf in North Korea]] does not exist. To specify a width for the icon and/or a particular variant/version of the flag, set the parameters {{para|size}} and/or {{para|variant}} in the same manner as {{tl|flag icon}}'s ''size'' and ''variant'' parameters. (See [[Template:Flag icon/doc|{{tlf|flag icon}}'s documentation]] for more details.) ===Example use in articles=== For examples of this template's use in articles, see the following articles: * [[Cabbage#Production]] * [[Administrative divisions of South America#Table]] (leftmost columns) {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{lua|Module:Flag}} {{Expensive}} == Description == {{flag+link|string|country|variant|size=|name=}} Produces a flag icon attached to a wikilinked article. This template is similar to the more widely used {{tl|flag}} and {{tl|flag country}} templates, but is more useful for representing something like a country/region/place/organization/etc followed by a link, labelled using that country/region/etc's name, to an article on some aspect of that country/region/etc. ;Parameters *{{para|1}} (first unnamed parameter): Use a string that will precede the country name defined in the second parameter. *{{para|2}} (second unnamed parameter): Use either the name of the state or in some cases, a three-letter country code (e.g. [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]]) *{{para|variant}} (optional parameter): Specifies an alternative (historical) flag to use. This parameter is documented by the appropriate template in [[:Category:Country data templates]]. *{{para|size}} (optional): Can be used to change the flag icon size. *{{para|name}} (optional): The display text of the target wikilink is usually automatically generated, but can always be over-ridden with the name parameter. ==Usage examples== # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} #:In this case, the country code for Liechtenstein (LIE) is used to produce the correct flag and wikilink to [[Politics of Liechtenstein]]. # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} #:Similar to the first example, but with a flag variant parameter to select a historical flag image. #{{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} → {{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} #:Optionally, an alternate name can be displayed. Note that the wikilink still uses the name used in the country's article (defined in [[Template:Country data Czech Republic]] in this case). #{{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} → {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} #:If no article exists with the name formed by the combination of ''string'' and ''name'', the link will default to [[''name'']]. {{tlx|flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}}, for instance, produces {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} with "North Korea" as a link to [[:en:North Korea|North Korea]] because [[Disc golf in North Korea]] does not exist. To specify a width for the icon and/or a particular variant/version of the flag, set the parameters {{para|size}} and/or {{para|variant}} in the same manner as {{tl|flag icon}}'s ''size'' and ''variant'' parameters. (See [[Template:Flag icon/doc|{{tlf|flag icon}}'s documentation]] for more details.) ===Example use in articles=== For examples of this template's use in articles, see the following articles: * [[Cabbage#Production]] * [[Administrative divisions of South America#Table]] (leftmost columns) {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{lua|Module:Flag}} {{Expensive}} == Description == {{flag+link|string|country|variant|size=|name=}} Produces a flag icon attached to a wikilinked article. This template is similar to the more widely used {{tl|flag}} and {{tl|flag country}} templates, but is more useful for representing something like a country/region/place/organization/etc followed by a link, labelled using that country/region/etc's name, to an article on some aspect of that country/region/etc. ;Parameters *{{para|1}} (first unnamed parameter): Use a string that will precede the country name defined in the second parameter. *{{para|2}} (second unnamed parameter): Use either the name of the state or in some cases, a three-letter country code (e.g. [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]]) *{{para|variant}} (optional parameter): Specifies an alternative (historical) flag to use. This parameter is documented by the appropriate template in [[:Category:Country data templates]]. *{{para|size}} (optional): Can be used to change the flag icon size. *{{para|name}} (optional): The display text of the target wikilink is usually automatically generated, but can always be over-ridden with the name parameter. ==Usage examples== # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} #:In this case, the country code for Liechtenstein (LIE) is used to produce the correct flag and wikilink to [[Politics of Liechtenstein]]. # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} #:Similar to the first example, but with a flag variant parameter to select a historical flag image. #{{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} → {{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} #:Optionally, an alternate name can be displayed. Note that the wikilink still uses the name used in the country's article (defined in [[Template:Country data Czech Republic]] in this case). #{{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} → {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} #:If no article exists with the name formed by the combination of ''string'' and ''name'', the link will default to [[''name'']]. {{tlx|flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}}, for instance, produces {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} with "North Korea" as a link to [[:en:North Korea|North Korea]] because [[Disc golf in North Korea]] does not exist. To specify a width for the icon and/or a particular variant/version of the flag, set the parameters {{para|size}} and/or {{para|variant}} in the same manner as {{tl|flag icon}}'s ''size'' and ''variant'' parameters. (See [[Template:Flag icon/doc|{{tlf|flag icon}}'s documentation]] for more details.) ===Example use in articles=== For examples of this template's use in articles, see the following articles: * [[Cabbage#Production]] * [[Administrative divisions of South America#Table]] (leftmost columns) {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{lua|Module:Flag}} {{Expensive}} == Description == {{flag+link|string|country|variant|size=|name=}} Produces a flag icon attached to a wikilinked article. This template is similar to the more widely used {{tl|flag}} and {{tl|flag country}} templates, but is more useful for representing something like a country/region/place/organization/etc followed by a link, labelled using that country/region/etc's name, to an article on some aspect of that country/region/etc. ;Parameters *{{para|1}} (first unnamed parameter): Use a string that will precede the country name defined in the second parameter. *{{para|2}} (second unnamed parameter): Use either the name of the state or in some cases, a three-letter country code (e.g. [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]]) *{{para|variant}} (optional parameter): Specifies an alternative (historical) flag to use. This parameter is documented by the appropriate template in [[:Category:Country data templates]]. *{{para|size}} (optional): Can be used to change the flag icon size. *{{para|name}} (optional): The display text of the target wikilink is usually automatically generated, but can always be over-ridden with the name parameter. ==Usage examples== # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} #:In this case, the country code for Liechtenstein (LIE) is used to produce the correct flag and wikilink to [[Politics of Liechtenstein]]. # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} #:Similar to the first example, but with a flag variant parameter to select a historical flag image. #{{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} → {{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} #:Optionally, an alternate name can be displayed. Note that the wikilink still uses the name used in the country's article (defined in [[Template:Country data Czech Republic]] in this case). #{{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} → {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} #:If no article exists with the name formed by the combination of ''string'' and ''name'', the link will default to [[''name'']]. {{tlx|flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}}, for instance, produces {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} with "North Korea" as a link to [[:en:North Korea|North Korea]] because [[Disc golf in North Korea]] does not exist. To specify a width for the icon and/or a particular variant/version of the flag, set the parameters {{para|size}} and/or {{para|variant}} in the same manner as {{tl|flag icon}}'s ''size'' and ''variant'' parameters. (See [[Template:Flag icon/doc|{{tlf|flag icon}}'s documentation]] for more details.) ===Example use in articles=== For examples of this template's use in articles, see the following articles: * [[Cabbage#Production]] * [[Administrative divisions of South America#Table]] (leftmost columns) {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23410180721|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{lua|Module:Flag}} {{Expensive}} == Description == {{flag+link|string|country|variant|size=|name=}} Produces a flag icon attached to a wikilinked article. This template is similar to the more widely used {{tl|flag}} and {{tl|flag country}} templates, but is more useful for representing something like a country/region/place/organization/etc followed by a link, labelled using that country/region/etc's name, to an article on some aspect of that country/region/etc. ;Parameters *{{para|1}} (first unnamed parameter): Use a string that will precede the country name defined in the second parameter. *{{para|2}} (second unnamed parameter): Use either the name of the state or in some cases, a three-letter country code (e.g. [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]]) *{{para|variant}} (optional parameter): Specifies an alternative (historical) flag to use. This parameter is documented by the appropriate template in [[:Category:Country data templates]]. *{{para|size}} (optional): Can be used to change the flag icon size. *{{para|name}} (optional): The display text of the target wikilink is usually automatically generated, but can always be over-ridden with the name parameter. ==Usage examples== # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} #:In this case, the country code for Liechtenstein (LIE) is used to produce the correct flag and wikilink to [[Politics of Liechtenstein]]. # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} #:Similar to the first example, but with a flag variant parameter to select a historical flag image. #{{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} → {{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} #:Optionally, an alternate name can be displayed. Note that the wikilink still uses the name used in the country's article (defined in [[Template:Country data Czech Republic]] in this case). #{{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} → {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} #:If no article exists with the name formed by the combination of ''string'' and ''name'', the link will default to [[''name'']]. {{tlx|flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}}, for instance, produces {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} with "North Korea" as a link to [[:en:North Korea|North Korea]] because [[Disc golf in North Korea]] does not exist. To specify a width for the icon and/or a particular variant/version of the flag, set the parameters {{para|size}} and/or {{para|variant}} in the same manner as {{tl|flag icon}}'s ''size'' and ''variant'' parameters. (See [[Template:Flag icon/doc|{{tlf|flag icon}}'s documentation]] for more details.) ===Example use in articles=== For examples of this template's use in articles, see the following articles: * [[Cabbage#Production]] * [[Administrative divisions of South America#Table]] (leftmost columns) {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Flag%2Blink%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{lua|Module:Flag}} {{Expensive}} == Description == {{flag+link|string|country|variant|size=|name=}} Produces a flag icon attached to a wikilinked article. This template is similar to the more widely used {{tl|flag}} and {{tl|flag country}} templates, but is more useful for representing something like a country/region/place/organization/etc followed by a link, labelled using that country/region/etc's name, to an article on some aspect of that country/region/etc. ;Parameters *{{para|1}} (first unnamed parameter): Use a string that will precede the country name defined in the second parameter. *{{para|2}} (second unnamed parameter): Use either the name of the state or in some cases, a three-letter country code (e.g. [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]]) *{{para|variant}} (optional parameter): Specifies an alternative (historical) flag to use. This parameter is documented by the appropriate template in [[:Category:Country data templates]]. *{{para|size}} (optional): Can be used to change the flag icon size. *{{para|name}} (optional): The display text of the target wikilink is usually automatically generated, but can always be over-ridden with the name parameter. ==Usage examples== # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE}} #:In this case, the country code for Liechtenstein (LIE) is used to produce the correct flag and wikilink to [[Politics of Liechtenstein]]. # {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} → {{flag+link|Politics of|LIE|variant=1719}} #:Similar to the first example, but with a flag variant parameter to select a historical flag image. #{{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} → {{flag+link|Sport in|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}} #:Optionally, an alternate name can be displayed. Note that the wikilink still uses the name used in the country's article (defined in [[Template:Country data Czech Republic]] in this case). #{{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} → {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} #:If no article exists with the name formed by the combination of ''string'' and ''name'', the link will default to [[''name'']]. {{tlx|flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}}, for instance, produces {{flag+link|Disc golf in|North Korea}} with "North Korea" as a link to [[:en:North Korea|North Korea]] because [[Disc golf in North Korea]] does not exist. To specify a width for the icon and/or a particular variant/version of the flag, set the parameters {{para|size}} and/or {{para|variant}} in the same manner as {{tl|flag icon}}'s ''size'' and ''variant'' parameters. (See [[Template:Flag icon/doc|{{tlf|flag icon}}'s documentation]] for more details.) ===Example use in articles=== For examples of this template's use in articles, see the following articles: * [[Cabbage#Production]] * [[Administrative divisions of South America#Table]] (leftmost columns) {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
